name: I refused light duty after my knee injury, will they cut my benefits in West Jordan?
description: Yes, benefits can be denied for refusing appropriate light duty in UT.[1]

mainEntity.name: I refused light duty after my knee injury, will they cut my benefits in West Jordan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, benefits can be denied for refusing appropriate light duty in UT.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, refusing medically appropriate light duty in Utah allows denial of temporary benefits during refusal.[1] Employers offer it to reduce premiums; discuss unfit duty with adjuster first.[1] Key factors: (1) Must fit doctor's restrictions; (2) Non-cooperation grounds denial; (3) Resolve issues before refusing; (4) Document all communications.[1] Confirm duty appropriateness with doctor, negotiate with insurer promptly. Refusal risks lost income—proceed cautiously.
